scenario: Submit requirement with all mandatory fields and valid values
preconditions: Application is available; user has access to requirement submission interface/API
steps: Open requirement submission page or API endpoint
Enter a complete valid requirement statement
test_data: Requirement text: 'User can reset password via OTP and email link.'
expected_result: Request is accepted and processed successfully with confirmation/200 response.

scenario: Generate test cases covering happy path for provided requirement
preconditions: Requirement is valid and parsing engine is operational
expected_result: Generated output includes functional happy-path test cases relevant to requirement.

scenario: Generate negative test cases for valid requirement
expected_result: Output contains invalid-input and rejection-path test cases.

scenario: Generate edge and boundary value test cases
expected_result: Output includes min-1, min, min+1, max-1, max, max+1 boundary cases.

scenario: Classify each test case with severity and priority
expected_result: Every test has valid 'priority' (P0-P3) and 'severity' (Major/Minor).

scenario: Output adheres to strict JSON schema
steps: Validate output against required fields and types
expected_result: Output is valid JSON array with required keys for each object.

scenario: Submit empty requirement
expected_result: System returns validation error indicating requirement is mandatory.

scenario: Submit requirement with only whitespace
expected_result: Request is rejected with mandatory-field validation message.

scenario: Submit non-English or mixed-language requirement
expected_result: System either processes correctly or returns clear unsupported-language error.

scenario: Submit malformed payload to API
expected_result: API returns 400 with parse/validation error and no crash.

scenario: Submit unsupported data type for requirement
expected_result: Validation error returned specifying expected string input.

scenario: Requirement at minimum allowed character length
expected_result: Request accepted and processed.

scenario: Requirement one character below minimum length
expected_result: Request rejected with min-length validation error.

scenario: Requirement at maximum allowed character length
expected_result: Request accepted without truncation or corruption.

scenario: Requirement one character above maximum length
expected_result: Validation error returned; system remains stable.

scenario: Requirement with special characters and punctuation
expected_result: Parser handles symbols safely; meaningful tests generated or clear warning shown.

scenario: Requirement with ambiguous wording
expected_result: Output includes assumptions or generic cases without failing silently.

scenario: Requirement with contradictory constraints
test_data: 'Password length must be exactly 8 and at least 12'
expected_result: System flags contradiction or produces explicit conflict note.

scenario: Internal processing failure while generating test cases
preconditions: Fault injection or mock failure enabled
steps: Trigger generation with induced backend exception
expected_result: Graceful error message/5xx returned with no sensitive stack trace leak.

scenario: Timeout during generation
expected_result: Request times out with controlled message and retry guidance.

scenario: SQL injection payload in requirement field
expected_result: Input is treated as plain text; no query manipulation or data impact.

scenario: XSS payload in requirement field
steps: View requirement and generated output in UI
expected_result: Payload is escaped/sanitized; script never executes.

scenario: Command injection style payload
preconditions: System may call shell or external tools
expected_result: Input is neutralized; no command execution occurs.

scenario: Unauthorized API access
expected_result: 401/403 returned; no data disclosed.

scenario: Excessive request rate (rate limiting)
steps: Send burst of generation requests above threshold
expected_result: 429 responses after threshold; service remains available.

scenario: Generation latency for normal-sized requirement
expected_result: Response time meets SLA for standard workload.

scenario: Generation under concurrent users
expected_result: System remains stable within acceptable latency and error thresholds.

scenario: Memory usage with very large requirement text
steps: Submit near-maximum or oversized requirement repeatedly
expected_result: No memory leak; resources return to baseline after requests complete.

scenario: Requirement containing unicode and emoji
test_data: 'ユーザーはログインできる 🔐'
expected_result: Text is preserved correctly; generation succeeds or returns clear limitation message.

scenario: Duplicate scenario prevention in output
expected_result: No duplicate test cases in final JSON output.

scenario: Unexpected user behavior: refresh/close during generation
expected_result: System recovers gracefully; no corrupted records or stuck requests.

scenario: Downstream dependency unavailable
expected_result: System returns controlled failure with retry/backoff, no crash, and actionable error.
